# OpenAlphaStack

OpenAlphaStack is an MIT-licensed Codex plugin for auditable A-share research,
backtesting, and paper trading. It combines reusable domain Skills, a typed
local MCP server, a deterministic Python engine, and a local read-only
Dashboard.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/44-99/OpenAlphaStack
Canonical Chinese page: https://44-99.github.io/OpenAlphaStack/
English discovery page: https://44-99.github.io/OpenAlphaStack/en.html

## Product boundaries

- Research, backtesting, and paper trading only.
- No live broker order tool and no guaranteed return claim.
- Codex Desktop owns conversation, scheduling, and research.
- Python owns deterministic validation, T+1 behavior, fees, state, and paper execution.
- The default workflow uses one Agent that composes domain Skills.
- The local Dashboard is not part of the public static website.
- The separately hosted public MCP is stateless and read-only; it cannot access local runs,
  portfolios, plans, ledgers, files, brokers, or order execution.

## Domain Skills

- market-analyzer: A-share market regime, sentiment, sectors, and leaders.
- stock-screener: deterministic screening and candidate review.
- stock-analyzer: technical, fundamental, news, position, and risk analysis.
- t0-intraday: position-based T0 feasibility and constraints.
